environment_option_used: real local PostgreSQL 18.2 (native Windows binary), matching the precedent from AKARFINDER-MARKET-INDEX-FOUNDATION-PROD-ACTIVATION-2/AKARFINDER-MARKET-INDEX-CONTROLLED-BACKFILL-1
engine: PostgreSQL 18.2 (native Windows binary, local, disposable data directory, ephemeral, dropped after the gate)
postgres_version_query_result: PostgreSQL 18.2 on x86_64-windows, compiled by msvc-19.44.35222, 64-bit

instance.auth: trust (local-only disposable instance, no password used, no network exposure beyond localhost)

role_model.grants: USAGE on schema public + SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on all 6 tables (2 base + 4 Market Index) granted to all three roles, matching Supabase's actual default grant pattern -- RLS is the real access boundary.

migration_apply_status: PASS -- all 5 applied cleanly, 0 errors (2 expected NOTICEs from 'drop constraint if exists' on constraints that did not pre-exist, harmless)

bug_found_and_fixed_before_any_productio.finding: discovery_candidates_idempotency_idx is a PARTIAL unique index (provider, query_hash, canonical_url) WHERE canonical_url IS NOT NULL. A plain upsert(onConflict: 'provider,query_hash,canonical_url') -- the pattern used everywhere else in this codebase for a simple unique constraint -- fails against a PARTIAL index: 'ERROR: there is no unique or exclusion constraint matching the ON CONFLICT specification'. This is a hard PostgreSQL requirement (the INSERT's own ON CONFLICT clause must declare the same predicate to use a partial index for conflict inference), not a PostgREST quirk, and reproduced identically against real PostgreSQL 18.2.
bug_found_and_fixed_before_any_productio.reproduction: confirmed via direct SQL: INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (provider, query_hash, canonical_url) DO UPDATE ... against the real migration-created partial index raises the exact error above.
bug_found_and_fixed_before_any_productio.fix: lib/openserp-ingestion/national-writer.ts's writeNationalDiscoveryCandidates() rewritten to a manual select-then-split-insert/update, keyed on the full (provider, query_hash, canonical_url) triple computed client-side -- proven idempotent and proven to correctly treat a different query_hash for the same canonical_url as a distinct row (2 rows, not 1, when query_hash differs) via a dedicated follow-up test.

test_3_anon_insert_rejected.actual: ERROR: new row violates row-level security policy for table "discovery_candidates"

test_4_authenticated_insert_rejected.actual: ERROR: new row violates row-level security policy for table "property_clusters"

test_5_service_role_full_writer_sequence.expected: service_role can write property_listings -> listing_sources -> property_clusters -> property_cluster_members in sequence (BYPASSRLS)
test_5_service_role_full_writer_sequence.actual: 1 property_listing, 1 listing_source (origin_type=persisted_openserp), 1 property_cluster (cluster_origin=deterministic_same_source_identifier), 1 property_cluster_member -- all created successfully

test_6_second_apply_idempotent.expected: rerunning the same writes produces 0 new property_listings/listing_sources/property_clusters, still updates mutable fields (title)
test_6_second_apply_idempotent.actual: counts unchanged (1/1/1), title updated to 'Appartement Maarif (v2)' confirming the on-conflict UPDATE branch ran, not a duplicate INSERT

test_7_anon_update_rejected.expected: UPDATE 0 rows (RLS blocks, no error since 0 rows matched is not itself an error)

test_8_one_to_one_membership_uniqueness.expected: a duplicate (property_cluster_id, source_offer_id) insert is silently skipped (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING), member count stays 1

test_9_fixed_discovery_candidates_path.expected: manual select-then-split: first run inserts, second run (same provider+query_hash+canonical_url) updates in place (count stays 1), a different query_hash for the same canonical_url correctly inserts a distinct row (count becomes 2)
